title: distimake cissoides
intro: Distimake cissoides, also known as Mexican tea or yerba de la reina, is a plant species native to Mexico and Central America, belonging to the Rubiaceae family. Traditionally, the leaves and stems of this plant have been used for their medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antibacterial properties. The health benefits of Distimake cissoides include treating digestive issues, sore throats, and skin irritations, as well as having antiviral and antifungal properties. Herbal preparations of Distimake cissoides can be made into teas, tinctures, and infusions for external and internal use, although more research is needed to fully understand its effects on human health.
